From the historic residences near St. George to the apartment complexes along Richmond Terrace, our team understands the specific pest challenges faced by New Brighton properties and delivers targeted solutions.

faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my New Brighton home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery, silken tubes or cases in fabric folds, adult moths flying near closets (especially at night), and larvae or cocoons in stored clothing. You may also notice damage to natural fiber rugs, blankets, or fur items. If you spot any of these signs in your New Brighton residence, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in New Brighton?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in New Brighton typically ranges from $550 to $5,000 depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of affected areas. A single-room treatment will cost less than whole-house fumigation. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any treatment, ensuring transparent pricing for all New Brighton customers.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on your property size. You'll see immediate reduction in adult moth activity, but complete elimination usually requires 2-3 follow-up treatments spaced 2-3 weeks apart to address newly hatched larvae. Most New Brighton infestations are fully resolved within 6-8 weeks with our comprehensive treatment protocol.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my New Brighton home?
faqs.answer: Yes! After treatment, we recommend regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing woolens in sealed containers, using cedar products or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and maintaining low humidity levels. We also offer preventative maintenance plans for New Brighton homes to catch any new activity early and provide ongoing protection for your valuable fabrics and garments.
